title: hieracium × byzantinum
intro: Hieracium × byzantinum, also known as the hawkweed, is a hybrid plant that is a cross between Hieracium vulgatum and Hieracium albinum, both members of the sunflower family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and aiding in the healing process of wounds and ulcers. Hieracium × byzantinum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
